ddls:
CREATE TABLE "production_orders" (
  "production_order_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each production order */,
  "order_number" TEXT /* Order number assigned to the production order */,
  "start_date_utc" TEXT /* Start date and time of the production order */,
  "end_date_utc" TEXT /* End date and time of the production order */,
  "article_code" TEXT /* Code of the article being produced */,
  "article_name" TEXT /* Name of the article being produced */,
  "segment_response_id" INTEGER /* ID of the segment response associated with the production order */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer placing the order */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the article ordered */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the production order (e.g., Pending, In Progress, Completed) */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Any additional notes or remarks about the order */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("production_order_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_production_orders_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customer ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_production_orders_segment_response_id FOREIGN KEY ("segment_response_id") REFERENCES production_segments ("segment_response_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "equipment" (
  "equipment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each piece of equipment */,
  "equipment_name" TEXT /* Name of the equipment */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the equipment */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the equipment (e.g., operational, maintenance) */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance performed on the equipment */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the equipment */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Serial number of the equipment */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the equipment */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date of installation */,
  "warranty_expiry_date" TEXT /* Date when the warranty expires */,
  "last_operational_check" TEXT /* Date and time of the last operational check */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("equipment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "production_segments" (
  "segment_response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each production segment response */,
  "production_request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the production request associated with the segment */,
  "start_date_utc" TEXT /* Start date and time of the segment */,
  "end_date_utc" TEXT /* End date and time of the segment */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the segment in seconds */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of the segment (e.g., RunTime, DownTime) */,
  "equipment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the equipment associated with the segment */,
  "operator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operator responsible for the segment */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Any additional notes or remarks about the segment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("segment_response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_production_segments_equipment_id FOREIGN KEY ("equipment_id") REFERENCES equipment ("equipment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_production_segments_production_request_id FOREIGN KEY ("production_request_id") REFERENCES production_orders ("production_order_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_production_segments_operator_id FOREIGN KEY ("operator_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reason_codes" (
  "reason_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reason code */,
  "reason_code" TEXT /* Code representing the reason for downtime */,
  "reason_description" TEXT /* Description of the reason for downtime */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the reason code (e.g., Mechanical, Electrical, Software) */,
  "suggested_action" TEXT /* Suggested action to resolve the issue */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reason_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "equipment_status" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status log entry */,
  "equipment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the equipment associated with the log */,
  "segment_response_id" INTEGER /* ID of the production segment response associated with the log */,
  "start_date_utc" TEXT /* Start date and time of the status log */,
  "end_date_utc" TEXT /* End date and time of the status log */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the status log in seconds */,
  "reason_id" INTEGER /* ID of the reason code for downtime */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of the status log (e.g., RunTime, DownTime) */,
  "operator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operator responsible for the status log */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Any additional notes or remarks about the status log */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_equipment_status_equipment_id FOREIGN KEY ("equipment_id") REFERENCES equipment ("equipment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_equipment_status_segment_response_id FOREIGN KEY ("segment_response_id") REFERENCES production_segments ("segment_response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_equipment_status_operator_id FOREIGN KEY ("operator_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_equipment_status_reason_id FOREIGN KEY ("reason_id") REFERENCES reason_codes ("reason_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user (hashed) */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., Admin, Operator, Maintenance) */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "customer_name" TEXT /* Name of the customer */,
  "contact_person" TEXT /* Name of the contact person */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the customer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)
